description_long: This object is a large solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a rectangular array of solar panels mounted on a robust metal framework. The panels are elevated above a patterned platform, which appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and composite materials, possibly for structural support and weather resistance. The solar array is supported by angled struts and beams, providing stability and optimal orientation for sunlight capture. The platform beneath the panels is visually striking, with geometric patterns in shades of black, gray, and white, and includes a ramped section at one end, suggesting possible vehicular or pedestrian access. The structure is open-sided, allowing for airflow and maintenance access beneath the panels. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are apparent, and the overall design is modern and utilitarian, suitable for urban environments such as parking lots, public plazas, or transit stations.
description: A large, elevated solar panel array on a patterned metal platform with angled support beams and a ramped access section. The structure is open-sided and visually modern, designed for urban solar energy collection.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the solar panel array, angled support beams, and the patterned platform beneath.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the ramped access section and the geometric pattern on the platform, with the solar panels overhead.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the solar array, support structure, and the platform's length and design details.
description_view_3: Reverse end view focusing on the entry side of the platform and the underside of the solar panels.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled steel or aluminum frame and open platform base
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 8-10 large panels mounted side by side
